author: @carlospenalver8721
text: Might be a coincidence but just about all the videos besides ones like this channel (TNT) are not only fakes but are made in countries where ex-pats move to because they can live off their pensions in a 10 bedroom home with 8 bathrooms so they can zhit all day while for the price of a pint (mug to you americanos) of beer you can order a maid who may be way too young for grey haired geezers like us to clean your house, let’s you tea bag her after the dishes are finished and when dinners done show you their countries version of a reverse cowgirl is. I mean if that guy making the fake battery had left the camera on and toured the town he lives in you would see little kids smoking vapes and playing online games for pennies in cryptos inside Internet cafes which aren’t at times even indoors at all, just a canopy covering an outdoor table with monitors behind heavy gauge steel grating boxes so the don’t steal them. I feel genuinely sorry for those there who are genuinely doing some research and training in actual fields who can’t get a leg up because scammers give everyone the impression that they’re just a state of quacks.

author: @VeniceInventors
text:
It comes down to  the motivation behind the actions. The misleading snake oil youtubers only care about money,, to fill the bottomless pit in their self-esteem from not knowing what they can contribute to the world, and couldn't care less about their audience, nor their impact on the rest of the world. 
Robert is the exact opposite, it's all about empowering the audience and spreading the ability to make a real difference at the same time.
Obviously nothing good will stem from the fake videos, on the other hand, many good things will stem from Robert's videos. It might not be visible in terms of views and subscribers, but someone somewhere on the other side of the planet did stumble upon a video about turning broken fans into windmills, and is building phone charging stations for destitute people, even though there is no money to be made in giving away free electricity, or making someone's life a little bit better, and yet, it is far more rewarding.

author: @dadbain
text: The one thing you failed to mention was there are different type of batteries. Though in the context of your review it's obvious you're speaking about a chemical battery. There are flow batteries, stored water batteries, artillery batteries and many more. That's the problem with the English language, the necessity to qualify the context of an ambiguous word. You'd be amazed at what seems common sensical evades some less complex minds. 🤔😊

author: @ChrisDavidge
text: It’s amazing how YouTube recommends these to us

author: @Bob.Jenkins
text:
I, too, was taught the 'fundamentals' of what a Battery is and what it 'must' contain... lately, however, I find myself querying those 'truths' simply because it may limit imagination and creativity.
Note: I'm *not* stating that the information I was taught is untrue - just that too steadfast a belief in a 'truth' can also be detrimental to Science, progress and innovation.
P.S. The video shown is obviously BS and considering its origin most likely a Scam.

author: @rowanshole
text: I havent checked it his video but I'm sure a lot of his viewers want the info only so they can maintain their sense of  indignity and outrage- "see, they can make big batteries cheap but they (always the" they") are ripping us off- look at this video where for penuts this guy proves it".

author: @scotttovey
text:
As morally bankrupt as that individual is; his audience is even more so, morally bankrupt.

They desire a quick and easy solution where there is not a quick and easy solution. That is probably because we are all basically lazy, wanting the easy way out rather than having to do the hard work that is necessary for finding true solutions to the problems.

Every industry has the overly optimistic dreamer that sells half truths to their audience; religion and politics perhaps being the two greatest violators.

Everyone wants to claim to be an expert despite not having expertise in an area, and the saddest part of this is that people tend to believe the liars, all the while rejecting those that speak the truth.

author: @Jarvis30
text:
What is the biggest catch phrase in marketing lately... "This 1 simple trick will....(insert pitch)"
Lower cholesterol 
Remove warts
Free energy
Boot testosterone 
Make money
Cure cancer
Remove stains
Get you a girlfriend 
Whatever they put in there people want a simple way to accomplish it.

author: @Jarvis30
text:
Bad videos like that are a good reason the thumbs down should be available to all viewers. If you leave a comment you help their algorithm and the bad info gets more views.  
Its just where our society is going.
I feel bad for the  younger and those new to thinking & tinkering that get this bad information seeded in their minds like its factual.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Would there be any electrical storage advantage to a barrel sized battery, from simple and available components, over an accumulation of smaller cells of the same mass? Some of his ingredients seemed (not watched the video) too elaborate and expensive for what he was pretending to try to do.  Suppose that he'd made an eg iron-carbon alkalai battery in the same size and weight, would it have been useful or just a waste of time and matterials.

replies.author: @paperburn
replies.text: yes, economy of scale, packing density to name a few BUT that was not a viable concept.

author: @CCoburn3
text: There are people who tell lies to get views. And their channels do well for a while. But eventually, they lose viewers. Still, they make money while it lasts.

replies.author: @billbimson2408
replies.text: Yeah, but then they start a new channel with a new set of lies and that money machine keeps on churning.  Also, a channel like that can go on for quite a while before people wise up to it and stop viewing.  I've been caught out myself viewing channels and investing readies in health supplements which don't appear to have worked like they say they do on the channels.  More fool me!

replies.author: @CCoburn3
replies.text: @billbimson2408 There are some computer channels that can get people to destroy their computers if they follow the presenter's advice. It's funny -- YouTube will ban a channel for telling an un-PC truth but will allow channels that do actual harm to continue.
